solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int findBestValue(vector<int>& arr, int target) {
        sort(arr.begin(), arr.end());
        int n = arr.size();
        vector<int> prefix(n + 1, 0);
        for (int i = 1; i <= n; ++i) {
            prefix[i] = prefix[i - 1] + arr[i - 1];
        }

        int left = 0, right = *max_element(arr.begin(), arr.end());
        int result = 0, minDiff = INT_MAX;

        while (left <= right) {
            int mid = (left + right) / 2;
            int idx = upper_bound(arr.begin(), arr.end(), mid) - arr.begin();
            int sum = prefix[idx] + mid * (n - idx);
            int diff = abs(sum - target);

            if (diff < minDiff) {
                minDiff = diff;
                result = mid;
            } else if (diff == minDiff && mid < result) {
                result = mid;
            }

            if (sum < target) {
                left = mid + 1;
            } else {
                right = mid - 1;
            }
        }

        return result;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses binary search to efficiently find the best value. The array is sorted, and a prefix sum array is built to quickly calculate the sum of elements less than or equal to a given value. Binary search is applied on the possible values, and for each mid value, the sum of the modified array is computed. The closest value to the target is tracked, with ties resolved by choosing the smaller value. The binary search ensures logarithmic time complexity, while sorting and prefix sum contribute to the overall efficiency.
